spec.title: Source adapter idempotent fact import contract

humanSummary.text: Initial audits found P1 blockers around self-attested import proofs, unstable durable marker identity, and already-imported checkpoint skips. Repair commits 2b27fc7, 6dabb16, and 60247ca fixed those blockers. The final focused re-audit found no remaining P0/P1/P2/P3 findings; earlier non-blocking audit findings were recorded as CATALOG-072 through CATALOG-076.

findings.title: Catalog replay verification can pass locally without DB-backed tests
findings.description: The DB package test command exits successfully with a skip artifact when DATABASE_URL is absent, while CATALOG-065 relies on DB-backed replay/idempotency behavior for its strongest proof.

evidence.detail: Independent audit noted that GitHub CI sets DATABASE_URL, but the public DAG command alone can be run locally without persisted replay tests executing.

findings.impact: Local verification can be mistaken for full persisted replay coverage when DB tests were skipped.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: A documented CATALOG-065 local gate runs replay repository tests against a disposable database.
Runs without DATABASE_URL emit an explicit machine-readable skipped artifact.

acceptanceCriteria: The documented CATALOG-065 local gate runs catalog replay repository tests against a disposable database.
existingDagNodeUpdate.notes: Follow-up node inserted in the same branch as audit finding ingestion.

findings.title: Replay identity coverage is narrow for multi-fact steps
findings.description: Current fixtures and tests focus on one-fact-per-step behavior, leaving multi-fact, duplicate identity, and count drift cases for later hardening.

evidence.detail: Audit compared replay fixture shape and fact-count assertions against future DLsite, Steam, IGDB, and Wikidata importer needs.

findings.impact: Future source adapters with several facts per crawler step could drift without targeted coverage.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: A multi-fact replay fixture persists each expected fact identity exactly once.
Duplicate source fact identities inside one step fail with semantic diagnostics.

acceptanceCriteria: Duplicate source fact identities inside one step fail with semantic diagnostics.
existingDagNodeUpdate.notes: Follow-up node inserted in the same branch as audit finding ingestion.

findings.title: Crash-window replay should use runner failure injection
findings.description: CATALOG-065 now verifies persisted evidence before checkpoint advance, but a future hardening node should exercise a real runner failure injection between ingest and commit instead of relying only on seeded state.

evidence.detail: Earlier audit flagged manually seeded pre-crash rows as weaker than a runner-level crash hook.

findings.impact: The exact crash window can regress if only manually arranged state is tested.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: A forced crash between fact ingestion and commitStepImport can be replayed without duplicate facts.
The test uses runner hooks rather than manually seeding rows outside the ingest path.

acceptanceCriteria: A forced crash between fact ingestion and commitStepImport can be replayed without duplicate facts.
existingDagNodeUpdate.notes: Follow-up node inserted in the same branch as audit finding ingestion.

findings.title: Catalog idempotency docs should avoid overclaiming generic crawler safety
findings.description: Docs should state that crash replay is safe when importers implement and verify the declared persisted evidence strategy, not because the generic crawler alone provides safety.

evidence.detail: Audit found wording that could be read as a generic guarantee rather than a strategy-specific contract.

findings.impact: Future adapter specs may cite ambiguous docs and omit strategy-specific persisted evidence.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Docs do not imply the generic crawler alone makes crash replay safe.
Each strategy example names the persisted evidence required before checkpoint advance.

acceptanceCriteria: Each strategy example names the persisted evidence required before checkpoint advance.
existingDagNodeUpdate.notes: Follow-up node inserted in the same branch as audit finding ingestion.

findings.title: Replay validation is not yet a durable artifact
findings.description: Replay validation exists in runner/test behavior but does not yet emit a durable artifact or table that adapter acceptance can audit outside the process.

evidence.detail: Audit requested source id, fixture id, stable import key, fact count, and fact identities as durable replay evidence.

findings.impact: Adapter acceptance depends on test logs rather than reusable validation evidence.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: A replay run emits durable validation evidence with source id, fixture id, stable import key, fact count, and fact identities.

acceptanceCriteria: A replay run emits durable validation evidence with source id, fixture id, stable import key, fact count, and fact identities.
existingDagNodeUpdate.notes: Follow-up node inserted in the same branch as audit finding ingestion.
